mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 6% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in real disposable personal income signals an increase in consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising real disposable personal income are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in real disposable personal income and a 3-month decline in the housing market signal an increase in consumer spending and a decrease in housing market activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 > 0 in 3 months and HOUST < 0 in 3 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real disposable personal income and declining housing market activity are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in payroll employment signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining payroll employment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the real stock of inventories signals an increase in corporate profitability and investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising real stock of inventories are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
